review.citation: Chun DI, et al. Diagnostic Accuracy of Radiologic Methods for Ankle Syndesmosis Injury: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis. Journal of Clinical Medicine. 2019.

identity.title: Diagnostic Accuracy of Radiologic Methods for Ankle Syndesmosis Injury: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

question.naturalLanguageInput: How accurately do radiologic imaging methods diagnose ankle syndesmosis injury?
question.structuredInput: In adults with suspected acute or chronic ankle syndesmosis injury, evaluate plain radiography, computed tomography, magnetic resonance imaging, or ultrasonography against arthroscopy, intraoperative findings, or an acceptable imaging reference standard; prioritize diagnostic accuracy studies.
eligibilityCriteria: Adults with suspected ankle syndesmosis injury
X-ray, CT, MRI, or ultrasonography used as an index test
Arthroscopy, intraoperative findings, or an acceptable reference standard
